highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.132 Released
highlights.summary: This release adds the `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` environment variable to Bash tool subprocesses, making it easier to track sessions in hooks and automation scripts. It also introduces `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_ALTERNATE_SCREEN=1` for developers who prefer the conversation to stay inline rather than switching to a fullscreen renderer. Both changes improve control over how Claude Code behaves in custom terminal environments.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.131 Released
highlights.summary: This patch fixes a critical bug that prevented the VS Code extension from activating on Windows due to a hardcoded build path issue. It also resolves a Mantle endpoint authentication failure caused by a missing `x-api-key` header. Windows users who experienced a broken extension should update immediately.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest addition to their flagship model family, bringing enhanced capabilities for complex coding and reasoning tasks. Developers using Claude Code can expect improved performance on large, multi-step coding problems. Check Anthropic's blog for details on how to access the new model via Claude Code's `/model` picker.

highlights.title: Uber Torches 2026 AI Budget on Claude Code in Four Months
highlights.summary: Reports indicate Uber burned through its entire 2026 AI tooling budget on Claude Code usage in just four months, highlighting how quickly costs can escalate with agentic AI coding tools at enterprise scale. This is a cautionary tale for teams adopting Claude Code without usage guardrails or budget caps. Developers should explore rate limiting, session scoping, and prompt efficiency techniques to keep costs manageable.

highlights.title: Claude Code Tutorial for Beginners
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial is a beginner-friendly walkthrough covering how to get started with Claude Code from scratch. It covers essential workflows like opening a project, making requests, and reviewing changes before accepting them. A great starting point if you are new to AI-assisted coding in VS Code.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This compact YouTube tutorial promises to take developers from basics to productive workflows in just 30 minutes, making it ideal for busy developers who want a fast ramp-up. It likely covers key commands, project setup, and tips for getting the most out of Claude Code sessions. A solid choice if you have already installed Claude Code and want to level up quickly.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.129 Released
highlights.summary: Version 2.1.129 adds a `--plugin-url` flag so you can load a plugin directly from a URL without downloading it manually first, streamlining plugin-based workflows. It also introduces `CLAUDE_CODE_FORCE_SYNC_OUTPUT=1` for users on non-standard terminals like Emacs `eat` that miss auto-detection. These additions make Claude Code more flexible in diverse development environments.

highlights.title: DeepClaude – Claude Code Agent Loop with DeepSeek V4 Pro
highlights.summary: DeepClaude is an open-source project that combines Claude Code's agentic loop with DeepSeek V4 Pro as the underlying reasoning model, offering a potential cost-saving alternative for developers on tight budgets. This kind of hybrid setup is made possible by Claude Code's support for custom `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` gateways. Intermediate to advanced users comfortable with environment configuration may find this a compelling experiment.

tips_of_the_day: Set a monthly token budget alert in your Anthropic console or CI pipeline before rolling out Claude Code to your whole team — enterprise usage can spike faster than expected, as Uber's experience shows.
If you are on Windows and your Claude Code VS Code extension suddenly stopped working, run `code --force-disable-user-env-probe` and then update to v2.1.131 or later to resolve the activation bug.

Use `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_ALTERNATE_SCREEN=1` in your shell profile if you prefer Claude Code's output to stay in your normal terminal scroll buffer instead of switching to fullscreen mode — great for logging sessions to a file.
